name: Fresh Peach Ice Cream
recipeIngredient: 3 medium ripe peaches; peeled, pitted, and cut into 1/2-inch pieces (about 2 cups)
1 1/4 cups whole milk (I used 2/3 cup milk and added half-and-half to make up the difference)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine peaches, lemon juice, salt, and 1/2 cup sugar in a nonreactive saucepan. Let stand until syrup forms and peaches soften slightly, about 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
2. Strain the peach mixture over a medium bowl set in an ice-water bath. Heat milk, cream, and 1/2 cup sugar in a heavy saucepan over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until steam appears, about 5 to 6 minutes. Turn off heat.
3. Whisk egg yolks and remaining sugar in a bowl until pale yellow. Stir half the warmed mixture into the yolks, then return to the saucepan with the remaining liquid. Heat gently, stirring constantly, until a thermometer registers 180°F (do not boil). Remove from heat and strain into the prepared bowl. Cool to room temperature, stir in vanilla, cover, and refrigerate until 40°F, at least 2 hours.
4. Meanwhile, heat softened peaches and their juices over medium-high heat until tender, about 3 to 4 minutes. Transfer to a bowl, add vodka, and chill until cold, at least 4 hours.
5. Strain chilled peaches and pour the liquid into the custard mixture. Churn in an ice cream machine according to manufacturer's instructions until it reaches soft-serve consistency, about 25 to 30 minutes. Add peaches and continue churning until combined. Transfer to an airtight container and freeze until firm, about 2 hours.
